title: Terahertz Landau level spectroscopy of Dirac fermions in millimeter-scale twisted bilayer graphene

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies millimeter-scale twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) with twist angles from about 7° to 20° using combined Hall transport and time-domain terahertz magneto-optics. By fitting complex Faraday rotation and Hall data, the authors extract cyclotron masses, chemical potentials, and Fermi velocities, showing that Dirac-like Landau-level physics persists well away from charge neutrality and that the measured velocities agree with prior STM/transport benchmarks.

figures.description: Calculated spectral function for a 9.43° TBG tight-binding model, showing the electronic band structure near the Dirac regime.
Calculated density of states with a chemical potential placed according to the 9° experimental sample.
Representative Hall resistance asymmetry versus magnetic field used to extract hole carrier density for the 9° sample.
Schematic zoom from the band structure to Landau-level spacing, illustrating the terahertz cyclotron resonance scale.
Experimental terahertz Faraday-rotation setup, including beam path, polarizers, delay stage, and detector geometry.
Real part of the complex Faraday rotation versus frequency and magnetic field for 7°, 9°, 10°, and 20° samples, with fits overlaid.
Imaginary part of the complex Faraday rotation versus frequency and magnetic field for the same samples, with fits overlaid.
Extracted cyclotron frequency versus magnetic field, fit to obtain cyclotron mass and disorder parameter.
Extracted scattering rate versus magnetic field from the Faraday-rotation fits.
Fermi velocity versus twist angle, comparing this work to prior STM and quantum-oscillation studies and to theory curves.
Raw Hall resistance data versus magnetic field for mono/7°/9°/10°/20° samples, shifted so Rxy(0)=0.
Asymmetric Hall resistance versus magnetic field with linear fits used to extract carrier density.
Tight-binding spectral function and density of states for θ(21,17)=6.96° sample, with chemical potential marked.
Tight-binding spectral function and density of states for θ(17,13)=8.80° sample, with chemical potential marked.
Tight-binding spectral function and density of states for θ(19,14)=10.00° sample, with chemical potential marked.
Tight-binding spectral function and density of states for θ(15,8)=19.93° sample, with chemical potential marked.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include time-domain terahertz electric-field traces Ex(t) and Ey(t) transmitted through the TBG samples under magnetic field, plus Hall resistance traces Rxy(B) and Rxx(B) measured in van der Pauw geometry at 2 K. The terahertz system uses femtosecond-laser-driven photoconductive antennas and records electric field directly in the time domain before Fourier transform.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include Fourier-transformed complex Faraday rotation spectra θF(ω), fitted cyclotron resonance frequencies ωc(B), scattering rates Γ(B), extracted carrier densities from antisymmetrized Hall data, and derived Fermi velocities versus twist angle. The paper also shows model-derived spectral functions and densities of states from tight-binding calculations.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, or file release is given in the provided text. The paper instead describes analysis methods, appendices, and tabulated extracted parameters.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the measurement level: time-domain terahertz electric fields measured by a detector antenna, and Hall resistance measured in a van der Pauw geometry. No file formats, filenames, or public raw-data archive are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include antisymmetrized Hall resistance, Fourier-transformed Faraday spectra, fitted resonance parameters, extracted carrier densities, cyclotron masses, chemical potentials, Fermi velocities, and tabulated values in Table I. The paper also provides model-generated spectral functions and DOS plots in the main text and supplement.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are shown extensively, including main-text figures and supplementary figures. However, the text does not state that separate source-data files are provided for each figure.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: Mixed and figure-centric in presentation; organization of any underlying files is not specified.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper provides enough plotted/derived values, equations, and tabulated parameters to reproduce many figures conceptually, but it does not explicitly provide downloadable source-data files or raw datasets. Replotting from the paper alone would require digitization or reimplementation of the analysis.
dataset_characterization.notes: Important distinction: the terahertz detector measures electric field directly, so the Faraday angle is computed from Ex and Ey and is not an intensity-only measurement. The paper’s Appendix C provides numerical extracted parameters and uncertainties, which are effectively processed/derived data rather than raw measurements. Tight-binding spectral functions and DOS are fully model-generated, not experimental data.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods explicitly describe direct measurement of time-domain terahertz electric fields and Hall resistance traces, and Appendix A shows raw Hall resistance data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The text does not mention separate source-data files or a data repository for figure reproduction.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Appendix A: Hall Effect Analysis; Appendix B: Tight-Binding Results; Appendix C: Extended Data; 'The terahertz detector directly measures the terahertz electric field, not the intensity'; 'See Appendix A for full Hall effect data'; 'Here we provide the complete data necessary to support the conclusions drawn in the main text.'
priority_modalities: tight-binding spectral functions and density of states
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not confuse model-generated spectral functions/DOS with experimental raw data. The paper clearly contains raw transport curves and raw terahertz field measurements conceptually, but it does not explicitly state a public raw-data package or source-data archive. The most concrete dataset semantics are in the appendices and Table I, which are derived outputs from fitting and modeling.
